content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS, consisting of lowercase English letters. You can do the following operation **at most once** :


Choose a subsequence of the string SSS, then remove it from SSS and concatenate the subsequence at the end of the remaining string.
Find the **lexicographically minimum string** that can be obtained by performing this operation **at most once** on SSS.

Note that:


A string AAA is a subsequence of a string BBB if AAA can be obtained by deleting several (possibly, zero or all) characters from BBB.
Given two strings of the same length AAA and BBB, we say that AAA is lexicographically smaller than BBB if there exists an index iii such that A1=B1,A2=B2,‚Ä¶,Ai‚ąí1=Bi‚ąí1A_1=B_1,A_2=B_2,\ldots,A_{i-1}=B_{i-1}A1‚Äč=B1‚Äč,A2‚Äč=B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ai‚ąí1‚Äč=Bi‚ąí1‚Äč and  Ai<BiA_i\lt B_iAi‚Äč<Bi‚Äč.

Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the lexicographically minimum string which can be obtained from SSS by performing the given operation at most once.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters.
Sum of ‚ą£S‚ą£\lvert S \rvert‚ą£S‚ą£ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It is optimal to choose the subsequence bbb, then remove and concatenate the subsequence at the end of the remaining string aaaaaa. Hence the final string is aa+b=aabaa + b = aabaa+b=aab.
Test case 222: The given string is already lexicographically minimum.
Test case 333: It is optimal to choose the subsequence ccdefccdefccdef, then remove and concatenate the subsequence at the end of the remaining string bbbbbb. Hence the final string is bb+ccdef=bbccdefbb + ccdef = bbccdefbb+ccdef=bbccdef.
